steps.title: Map the passage's two-part structure
steps.content: The passage opens by citing two specific buildings as exemplars of a design trend, then explains the principles underlying that trend. The key signal is this two-part structure: concrete examples followed by explanation of underlying principles.

steps.title: Apply to the choices
steps.content: D is correct because it accurately describes what the passage does: it “cites examples of a design trend” (the two buildings) and “establishes the principles underlying the trend” (post-WWII technology emphasis and traditional preservation). A, B, and C all mischaracterize the second part — A points to historical origins, B to past material uses, and C to one aesthetic dominating the other. That matches choice D — D is correct.
